rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2023, line 1. Respondent's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is DENIED. Moving party fails to show that the entire first cause of action "To set aside the 2021 election and prohibit editing of candidate statements" is barred by 1 year statute of limitations. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MURKELLEY, LLC, VS. MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2023, line 3. Defendant Mission Housing Demurrer to Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action for declaratory relief not addressing past wrongs/breaches as suggested in opposition at 9:21-22 ("whether MHDC must remove the sublessees currently occupying the premises.") rulings.Case Title: MURKELLEY, LLC, VS. MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2023, line 3. Defendant Caritas Management Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the cause of action for interference with contractual relations is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges that Caritas was an agent of MHDS (e.g. paragraphs 9, 11, 12, 32) per Asahi Kasei Pharma Corp. v. Actelion Ltd. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945 cited by Plaintiff in opposition an agent of a party to the contract in not a "stranger" ("We agree with the Woods court that "[a] stranger," as used in Applied Equipment, means one who is not a party to the contract or an agent of a party to the contract."). Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action for declaratory relief not addressing past wrongs/breaches as suggested in opposition at 12:20-21. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FUNG LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Defendant Annie O'Keeffe
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2023, line 5. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Alternative requestion for a Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issues of fact exist as to the reasonableness of denial of the application to add an occupant. Furthermore, moving party failed to shift its burden and show that the appropriate form 1007 prepared by the rent board was served on the defendant. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the affirmative defense of waiver (no opposition filed); affirmative defense of failure to reasonably accommodate (motion for summary adjudication is framed by the pleadings. The operative answer was filed on April 20, 2023 asserting that Plaintiff refused to reasonably accommodate. No evidence presented in discovery or in opposition to this motion supporting the pleading's assertion that a reasonable accommodation was refused on or before April 20, 2023); affirmative defense of failure to state facts (this is not a cognizable affirmative defense); affirmative defense of laches and unclean hands (no opposition filed). Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to affirmative defenses for violation of SFRO/retailiation - triable issues of fact exist regarding just cause for eviction, reasonableness of additional occupant application denial and retaliation; affirmative defense of estoppel -triable issues of fact exist regarding Mr. Moore's occupancy/stay/living at the premises. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. SAMUEL CHANCEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2023, line 6. Mele Tavake's Claim of Right to Possession is DENIED. Proof of service on file in this case indicates that a pre-judgment claims of right to possession was served. Claimant failed to file any evidence (having been given an opportunity to do so on July 7, 2023) to show that Post-Judgment Claim or Right to possession is appropriate in this action. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
